# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

use Stance::{Implemented as Impl, Recognized as Rec, Unsupported as Unsup};

/// The full Appendix A configuration-key baseline.

# Dictionary source pipeline (stub — Stage S0)

This directory holds the **normalized TrueFix dictionary** inputs and the transform that produces them.
Per Constitution Principle III and spec FR-A2, dictionaries are **derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications (FIX Orchestra / Repository)** and normalized into
TrueFix's own format. QuickFIX/J `FIXxx.xml` files are **never** copied.

## Layout (to be populated in Stages S4/S7)

```
dict-src/
├── orchestra/      # vendored FIX Orchestra/Repository inputs (official, license-checked)
├── normalized/     # generated TrueFix normalized dictionaries (one per version)
└── generate.rs     # transform: orchestra -> normalized (invoked from build.rs)
```

## Dual-track usage (Principle IV)

The single `normalized/` output is consumed by:
- `build.rs` codegen → strongly-typed per-version messages (compile-time).
- the runtime `DataDictionary` validator (run-time).

A build assertion (T047) verifies both originate from the same normalized version/hash so they cannot
diverge.

## Status

S0 scaffold only. Population of `orchestra/` + the `generate` transform is tracked by tasks T048, T050,
T074, T075.

# TrueFix Codebase — Exhaustive Analysis (current state, no git history)

I read the actual source of **all 84 `.rs` files** under `crates/`, all 9 crate `Cargo.toml`s, the workspace `Cargo.toml`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `README.md`. Below is the complete current state.

## Workspace overview
- **9 crates**: `truefix-core`, `truefix-dict`, `truefix-session`, `truefix-store`, `truefix-log`, `truefix-transport`, `truefix-config`, `truefix-at`, `truefix` (facade). All `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`, edition 2021, MSRV 1.96.
- Workspace lints: `unsafe_code = "forbid"`; each lib.rs adds `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds.
- Shared deps: tokio(full), tokio-rustls 0.26, rustls 0.23, thiserror 2, rust_decimal, time 0.3, sqlx 0.8 (sqlite, optional), async-trait, tracing.
- NOTE: README badge says "pre-implementation (design complete)" but the code is **substantially implemented** through roughly S0–S9.

## (a) truefix-core — message model + SOH codec
Files: `lib.rs, error.rs, field.rs, field_map.rs, group.rs, message.rs, codec/{mod,decode,encode}.rs, framing.rs, cracker.rs, factory.rs, tags.rs` + `benches/codec.rs`.
- **`Field`** (`field.rs`): tag + raw `Vec<u8>` value (verbatim for round-trip). Typed accessors `as_str/as_int/as_decimal/as_char/as_bool/as_utc_timestamp`, all returning `Result<_, FieldError>` (never panic). Timestamp parser accepts `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS[.fraction]`, picosecond input truncated to nanosecond.
- **`FieldError`**: NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp. **`DecodeError`**: Empty, MissingBeginString, InvalidBodyLength, BodyLengthMismatch, MissingChecksum, ChecksumMismatch, GarbledField{offset,reason}, Truncated, InvalidTag.
- **`FieldMap`** (`field_map.rs`): ordered `Vec<Member>` where `Member = Field | Group{count_tag, entries: Vec<FieldMap>}`. Methods: `add_field`, `set` (replace/append), `get`, `contains`, `add_group`, `group`, `fields()`, `members()` (pub(crate)).
- **`Group`** (`group.rs`): count_tag + entries builder. NOTE: this lets you *build/encode* groups; **dictionary-driven *parsing* of groups from a flat wire message is NOT implemented** (core doc-comment defers it to truefix-dict Stage S4; dict's `validate.rs` likewise only walks flat `fields()`).
- **`Message`**: header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s. `encode()`/`decode()` delegate to codec. Canonical emit order `8,9,35,<header>,<body>,<trailer except 10>,10`.
- **codec/decode.rs**: tokenizes `tag=value<SOH>`, honors length-prefixed binary data fields (via `tags::data_field_for_length`), verifies BodyLength + CheckSum. Routes fields to header/body/trailer via `is_header`/`is_trailer`.
- **codec/encode.rs**: computes BodyLength + CheckSum, recurses into nested groups.
- **framing.rs** (`frame_length`): stream framing via BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer.
- **cracker.rs**: `MessageCracker` trait — **skeleton only, NOT typed dispatch**. Just `fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`. Doc says typed dispatch arrives with codegen Stage S4.
- **factory.rs**: `MessageFactory` trait — **skeleton only** (`fn create(&self, begin_string, msg_type) -> Message`).
- **tags.rs**: SOH=0x01, BEGIN_STRING=8, BODY_LENGTH=9, MSG_TYPE=35, CHECK_SUM=10, MSG_SEQ_NUM=34, SENDER_COMP_ID=49, TARGET_COMP_ID=56, SENDING_TIME=52. `is_header`/`is_trailer` classification + `data_field_for_length` map (SecureDataLen→SecureData, RawDataLength→RawData, XmlDataLen→XmlData, Encoded*Len→Encoded* pairs).

## (b) truefix-dict — dual-track data dictionary
Files: `lib.rs, build.rs, model.rs, validate.rs, parser.rs, fixt.rs, hash.rs` + `dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict` (9 dict files + README).
- **Versions supported**: FIX 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, **FIXT 1.1**, FIX 5.0, 5.0SP1, 5.0SP2. **NO "FIX Latest"** (no matches for fixlatest/FIX_Latest).
- **Build-time codegen** (`build.rs`): reads the 9 normalized `.fixdict` sources, emits per-version `{NAME}_DICT_HASH: u64` (FNV-1a, duplicated in `hash.rs`) + `pub mod {name}_msgs { pub const MSG_NAME: &str = ...; }` MsgType constants into `OUT_DIR/generated.rs`.
- **Runtime** (`parser.rs`): parses a custom normalized format — directives `version`, `field <tag> <name> <TYPE> [enumvals...]`, `header <tags>`, `trailer <tags>`, `message <msgtype> <name> [req:tag,tag] [opt:tag,tag]`, with `#` comments. `ParseError`: MissingVersion/Malformed{line}/UnknownType{line,token}.
- **`DataDictionary`** (`model.rs`): version, fields (BTreeMap<u32,FieldDef>), field_by_name, messages (BTreeMap<String,MessageDef>), header/trailer sets, hash. `FieldType` enum: Int/Length/SeqNum/NumInGroup/Float/Price/Qty/Amt/Percentage/Char/Boolean/String/Data/UtcTimestamp/UtcTimeOnly/UtcDateOnly/MonthYear. `FieldDef{tag,name,field_type,values}`. `MessageDef{msg_type,name,required,optional}`. **`RejectReason`** enum with codes: InvalidTagNumber(0), RequiredTagMissing(1), TagNotDefinedForMessageType(2), UndefinedTag(3), TagSpecifiedWithoutValue(4), ValueIsIncorrect(5), IncorrectDataFormat(6), InvalidMsgType(11). `ValidationError{reason,ref_tag,business,text}` (session vs business). `ValidationOptions`: validate_fields_have_values, validate_user_defined_fields, allow_unknown_msg_fields, check_required_fields, check_field_types.
- **validate.rs**: `DataDictionary::validate(&Message, &ValidationOptions)`. Walks flat header/body/trailer fields; UDFs (tag≥5000) skipped unless `validate_user_defined_fields`; unknown-msg-fields allowed if `allow_unknown_msg_fields`; checks value format, enum, field membership, required fields. Unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject; field failures → session reject. **Two rejection layers present.**
- **fixt.rs**: `FixtDictionaries` — transport dict + BTreeMap<appl_ver_id, DataDictionary> + default_appl_ver_id; `application_for()` resolves per-message.
- Tests: `versions.rs`, `parse.rs`, `fixt.rs`, `toggles.rs`, `rejection_layers.rs`, `dual_track.rs` (proves codegen hash == runtime hash).

## (c) truefix-session — session state machine
Files: `lib.rs, state.rs, application.rs, config.rs, session_id.rs, schedule.rs, admin.rs, tags.rs, time_util.rs`.
- **`Session`** (`state.rs`): **sans-IO** state machine. `Event` = Connected | Received(Message) | Tick | StartLogout. `Action` = Send(Message) | Disconnect. `SessionState` = Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout. `SessionStatus` snapshot (state, logged_on, next_sender_seq, next_target_seq).

- **`SessionConfig`** (`config.rs`): begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval(30), role, reset_on_logon(true)/reset_on_logout(false)/reset_on_disconnect(false), refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size(0), enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency(true), max_latency(120), logon_timeout(10), logout_timeout(10), reconnect_interval(5), schedule.
- **`SessionId`**: begin_string + sender/target + optional sub/location IDs + session_qualifier.
- **`Schedule`** (`schedule.rs`): non_stop / daily window (wraps past midnight) / weekday filter / whole-second UTC offset. `is_in_session(now_utc)`. Named TZ and StartDay/EndDay noted as future.
- **admin.rs**: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/`reject_with_reason`/`business_message_reject`/`prepare_resend` (stamps PossDupFlag + OrigSendingTime). **`time_util.rs`**: SendingTime always formatted at **millisecond** precision (`YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss`) — `TimeStampPrecision` key is Recognized but NOT honored.
- Tests: `state_machine.rs`, `recovery.rs`, `validation_hook.rs`, `timeouts.rs`, `schedule.rs`.

## (d) truefix-store — persistence
Files: `lib.rs, memory.rs, file.rs, noop.rs, sql.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default; sqlx sqlite).
- **`MessageStore`** async trait: `next_sender_seq`, `next_target_seq`, `set_next_sender_seq`, `set_next_target_seq`, `save(seq, &[u8])`, `get(begin, end) -> Vec<(u64, Vec<u8>)>`, `reset`. `StoreError` = Io | Backend.
- **`MemoryStore`**: `Mutex<State{sender,target,messages:BTreeMap}>`.
- **`FileStore`** (`file.rs`): `seqnums` file (sender/target on lines 1/2) + `body` append log (`seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`). On open replays body into memory; **tolerates/recoveres truncated/corrupt trailing record** and flags it via `was_corrupted()` (supports **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore** T060 — Recognized in keys, partial impl: corruption tolerated, good prefix recovered). **`CachedFileStore`** wraps FileStore (shared impl; "write-batching optimizations will live" here — not yet differentiated).
- **`NoopStore`**: always reports seq 1, discards saves.
- **`SqlStore`** (`sql.rs`, behind `sql` feature): SQLite via sqlx. Tables **hardcoded**: `seqnums` (id=0 singleton, sender/target), `messages` (seq PK, data BLOB). `StoreConfig::Sql{url}` + `build_store()`. **Table names NOT configurable** (Jdbc*TableName keys are Recognized but not honored); **no connection max-lifetime/idle/keepalive config** (JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime etc. Recognized but not honored — uses plain `SqlitePool::connect_with`).
- Tests: `restart_resend.rs`. **`FileStoreSync` key is Recognized but NOT honored** (FileStore uses synchronous `fs::write`, no fsync/sync toggle).

## (e) truefix-log — logging
Files: `lib.rs, screen.rs, file.rs, tracing_log.rs, composite.rs, sql.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default; sqlx sqlite + tokio).
- **`Log`** trait (sync, infallible): `on_incoming(&str)`, `on_outgoing(&str)`, `on_event(&str)` — message/event stream separation.
- Backends: **`ScreenLog`** (stdout msgs, stderr events), **`FileLog`** (`messages.log`+`event.log` in a dir), **`TracingLog`** (tracing facade, targets `truefix::message`/`truefix::event`), **`CompositeLog`** (fan-out), **`SqlLog`** (behind `sql` feature: writes to hardcoded `log_messages`/`log_events` tables via a background tokio task fed by an unbounded mpsc — bridges sync trait to async sqlx). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`.
- Tests: `composite.rs`, `sql_log.rs`. **SLF4J keys are Unsupported** ("TrueFix logs via tracing facade; SLF4J category keys not applicable").

## (f) truefix-transport — TCP/TLS initiator+acceptor
Files: `lib.rs, framing.rs` (transport has its own `frame_length`, duplicate of core's).
- **Initiator**: `connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with` (services), `connect_initiator_tls` (rustls `ClientConfig` + SNI `ServerName`), **`connect_initiator_reconnecting`** (auto-reconnect after `reconnect_interval`), **`run_scheduled_initiator`** (connects only while `Schedule.is_in_session`; on entering window resets store, on leaving logs out).
- **Acceptor**: simple `Acceptor` (one static config) + **`AcceptorBuilder`** (multi-session): `with_session` (static by SessionID), `with_dynamic_template` (dynamic sessions), `allow_remotes` (IP allow-list), `with_tls` (rustls `ServerConfig`), `with_services`. Routing by SessionID: reads first complete message (Logon), reverses comp IDs to look up config, falls back to dynamic template.
- **`Services`**: optional `store` (Arc<dyn MessageStore>), `log`, `socket_options` (tcp_no_delay only), `monitor`, `validator` (DataDictionary + ValidationOptions). On connect: restores seq from store, registers with monitor. Persists seq numbers best-effort after each dispatch.
- **`Monitor`**: live status (`status`, `is_connected`, `sessions`), plus control actions `force_logout`, `reset`, `send_app` — described in code as **"the JMX-equivalent surface"** (no actual JMX/MBean/JmxExporter).
- `Application::from_admin` Err tears the session down (auth/reject-logon). Garbled frames silently dropped ("RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" — not yet). `SessionHandle` control: `logout`, `send`, `join`.
- Tests: `scheduled.rs`, `monitor.rs`, `auth.rs`, `tls.rs`, `multi_dynamic.rs`, `restart_continuity.rs`, `reconnect.rs`, `integration_logon.rs`.
- **TLS**: caller supplies pre-built `Arc<rustls::ClientConfig/ServerConfig>`; transport does NOT parse `EnabledProtocols`/cipher/keystore itself (those keys are marked Implemented in keys.rs but no parsing in transport — min-version/raw-bytes config not wired).

## (g) truefix-config — .cfg parsing + Appendix A registry
Files: `lib.rs, keys.rs`.
- **`SessionSettings::parse`**: QuickFIX-style `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` dialect, `#` comments, per-session override of defaults, **`${name}` interpolation** (errors on unresolved). Returns default section + resolved session sections. NOTE: **parses only — there is NO mapping from settings → `SessionConfig`** yet.
- **`keys.rs`**: `APPENDIX_A_KEYS` registry (~150 keys) with `Stance` = `Implemented`/`Recognized`/`Unsupported(&'static reason)`. Groups: identity, validation, session, scheduling, acceptor, initiator, socket, ssl, proxy, file-store, file-log, screen-log, facade-log, sql, sleepycat. `key_info(name)` lookup. Test `key_coverage.rs` asserts ≥140 keys, no dups, every Unsupported has a non-empty reason.
- **Proxy keys** (ProxyType/ProxyHost/…): all **Unsupported** ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy not yet implemented"). **Sleepycat keys**: all **Unsupported** ("deferred for v1"). **SLF4J keys**: Unsupported. **JNDI keys**: Unsupported ("not applicable in Rust").

## (h) truefix-at — acceptance test suite
Files: `lib.rs, runner.rs, scenarios.rs` + `tests/conformance.rs`.
- **`runner.rs`**: black-box driver. Starts a real `AcceptorBuilder` acceptor (dynamic template, optional validator/executor app) on `127.0.0.1:0`, scripts a TCP client. `Step` = Send(Message)/SendRaw(bytes)/Expect(ExpectMsg{msg_type, fields})/ExpectDisconnect. `Scenario{name, versions, steps, tweaks: SessionTweaks}`. `SessionTweaks`: enable_next_expected, enable_last_processed, check_latency, resend_chunk_size, executor_app (replies to NewOrderSingle with ExecutionReport). `run_report()` runs each scenario × each target version, fresh acceptor per (scenario,version). Validator only loaded for "FIX.4.2" and "FIX.4.4".
- **`SUITE_VERSIONS = ["FIX.4.2", "FIX.4.4"]`** — AT scenarios run ONLY against these two versions (not all 9).
- **`scenarios.rs` — exact scenario list (45 unique, 66 scenario×version instances)**:
  - **Shared, run vs BOTH FIX.4.2 & FIX.4.4 (21 × 2 = 42)**: `1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum`, `1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2c_MsgSeqNumTooLow`, `4b_ReceivedTestRequest`, `13b_UnsolicitedLogoutMessage`, `1d_LogonResponseResetFlag`, `1c_LogonAdoptsHeartBtInt`, `2f_ResendRequestGapFill`, `2f3_ResendRequestBoundedEnd`, `2_ResendRequestNotDuplicated`, `2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored`, `2x_OutOfOrderQueuedThenDrained`, `2g_SequenceResetReset`, `2d_SequenceResetGapFillAdvances`, `2e_SequenceResetGapFillBackwardIgnored`, `2h_ResendRequestNothingToResend`, `3a_ReceivedRejectConsumed`, `0a_ReceivedHeartbeatConsumed`, `2_MissingMsgSeqNum`, `2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow`.
  - **FIX.4.2 only (7)**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue_42`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber_42`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType_42`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue_42`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat_42`.
  - **FIX.4.4 only — field-validation + app (12)**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted`, `app_NewOrderSingleExecuted`, `app_OrdersOutboundSequenced`, `app_MessageResentAsPossDup`, `app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat`, `2r_UnregisteredMsgType`.
  - **FIX.4.4 only — special suite (5)**: `special_NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, `special_LastMsgSeqNumProcessed`, `special_CheckLatencyStaleSendingTime`, `special_GarbledMessageDropped`, `special_ResendRequestChunkSize`.
- Categories: logon/sequencing, admin handling, resend/gap-fill, out-of-order queueing, field-validation reject family (14a–14f across 4.2 & 4.4), business reject (2r), app-execution/resend-as-PossDup, and special toggles (789/369, CheckLatency, garbled/checksum, chunk size).

## (i) truefix (facade)
`lib.rs` re-exports: core (`decode,encode,DecodeError,Field,FieldError,FieldMap,Group,Message,MessageCracker,MessageFactory`), session (`Action,Application,Event,Role,Session,SessionConfig,SessionId,SessionState`), transport (`connect_initiator,Acceptor,SessionHandle`), and `config` module. `#![deny(missing_docs)]`.
- **Examples (4)** in `crates/truefix/examples/`: `executor.rs` (acceptor that fills each NewOrderSingle with ExecutionReport, port 9881), `banzai.rs` (initiator client: logon→send NewOrderSingle→print ExecutionReport→logout), `multi_acceptor.rs` (two static sessions + dynamic template, port 9882), `ordermatch.rs` (simplified matching engine: per-symbol book, crossing orders filled on both sides, port 9883). Plus `tests/examples_smoke.rs`.

## Section 5 — specific feature checklist (verified by reading + grep)
- **failover/alternate/SocketConnectHost1/multi-endpoint/BackupHost**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches). Only single `SocketConnectHost`/`SocketConnectPort`.
- **proxy / PROXY protocol**: config keys present, **all Unsupported** ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy not yet implemented"). No PROXY-protocol support.
- **mongo/MongoDB**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches).
- **Sleepycat/berkeley**: keys present, **all Unsupported** ("deferred for v1; use file or SQL store").
- **fixlatest / FIX Latest**: **NOT PRESENT**. Versions are FIX 4.0–4.4, FIXT 1.1, FIX 5.0/SP1/SP2 only.
- **DoNotSend/RejectLogon/FieldNotFound (by name)**: not present as named concepts. RejectLogon behavior exists via `Application::from_admin` returning `Err` (tears session down). FieldNotFound maps to `RejectReason::InvalidTagNumber`/`RequiredTagMissing`.
- **UnsupportedMessageType/IncorrectTagValue**: map to `RejectReason::InvalidMsgType(11)` and `ValueIsIncorrect(5)`; `IncorrectDataFormat(6)` also exists.
- **MessageCracker**: **trait skeleton only, NOT typed dispatch** (cracker.rs doc says typed dispatch arrives with codegen Stage S4).
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter / ApplicationExtended**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **JMX/JmxExporter/MBean**: **NOT PRESENT** as actual JMX. `Monitor` is described in-code as "the JMX-equivalent surface" (status + force_logout/reset/send_app).
- **OSGi/bundle**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **ResetSeqTime**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **TimeStampPrecision**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT honored** — `time_util` always emits millisecond precision.
- **FileStoreSync**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT honored** — FileStore uses synchronous `fs::write`, no fsync/sync toggle.
- **HeartBtIntOverride**: **NOT PRESENT** (acceptor does adopt the peer's HeartBtInt from the Logon, but no override key).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: Recognized in keys.rs; **partial implementation** — FileStore detects corrupt trailing record, recovers the good prefix, exposes `was_corrupted()` (T060). No "force resend" behavior beyond recovery.
- **InChanCapacity**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **ConnectionValidator**: **NOT PRESENT** (auth via `from_admin` Err instead).
- **watermark/backpressure**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches).
- **SLF4J**: keys present, **Unsupported** ("TrueFix logs via tracing facade; not applicable").
- **dictgenerator / codegen CLI**: **NO standalone CLI**. `build.rs` does build-time codegen (MsgType constants + FNV-1a hash) only; no `dictgenerator` binary.
- **custom SQL table names**: **NOT configurable** — SqlStore/SqlLog hardcode `seqnums`/`messages`/`log_messages`/`log_events`. Jdbc*TableName keys are Recognized but not honored.
- **SQL connection max lifetime**: JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime Recognized, **NOT honored** (plain `SqlitePool::connect_with`).
- **TLS minimum version config / TLS raw bytes config**: `EnabledProtocols`/`CipherSuites`/`SocketKeyStore` etc. marked Implemented in keys.rs, but transport takes a **pre-built `Arc<rustls::ClientConfig/ServerConfig>`** from the caller — no protocol-version/cipher/keystore/PEM parsing in the transport crate.
- **virtual thread support**: N/A (Rust/tokio async; no such concept present).
- **ValidateUnorderedGroupFields**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT implemented** — group parsing from flat wire not yet done anywhere.
- **ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder**: Recognized, not implemented (decoder preserves wire order; no out-of-order rejection).

## Notable gaps / things not yet wired
1. **No settings→SessionConfig mapping** in truefix-config (parses `.cfg` only; engine uses programmatic `SessionConfig`).
2. **Group parsing from flat wire** deferred everywhere (core + dict validate walk flat fields only).
3. **RejectGarbledMessage** not implemented (garbled frames silently dropped in transport).
4. Many Appendix A keys are `Recognized` (parsed) but not behaviorally honored (TimeStampPrecision, FileStoreSync, FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs, RejectGarbledMessage, ResetOnError, PersistMessages, ClosedResendInterval, SendRedundantResendRequests, several socket/SSL options, all Jdbc tuning keys, screen-log toggles).
5. **No standalone dictgenerator CLI**, no JMX, no proxy, no Sleepycat, no MongoDB, no failover/multi-endpoint — all explicitly absent or Unsupported-with-reason.

## Evidence basis
All findings are from reading the current source: every `src/*.rs` in all 9 crates, all 9 `Cargo.toml`s, workspace `Cargo.toml`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `truefix-config/tests/key_coverage.rs`, plus grep verification across `crates/` for failover/mongo/fixlatest/JMX/OSGi/proxy/Sleepycat/Validate*/FileStoreSync/TimeStampPrecision/ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore. No git history was consulted.

Next steps:
The codebase is fully mapped. If the caller wants to act on this: (1) the largest functional gaps are settings→SessionConfig wiring, flat-wire group parsing, and honoring the many Recognized-but-not-implemented config keys (TimeStampPrecision, FileStoreSync, RejectGarbledMessage, Jdbc tuning, etc.); (2) explicitly-absent QF/J features (failover/multi-endpoint, proxy/PROXY protocol, MongoDB, Sleepycat, JMX, OSGi, ApplicationExtended, dictgenerator CLI) are documented Unsupported or simply missing — these would be net-new work, not wiring. No further file reading is required to begin any of these.

# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"

response.output:
# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

use Stance::{Implemented as Impl, Recognized as Rec, Unsupported as Unsup};

/// The full Appendix A configuration-key baseline.
pub const APPENDIX_A_KEYS: &[KeyInfo] = &[
    // Session identity & type
    k("BeginString", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderCompID", "identity", Impl),

    k("ScreenLogShowIncoming", "screen-log", Rec),
    k("ScreenLogShowOutgoing", "screen-log", Rec),

    // Facade (SLF4J-equivalent) log

/// Look up the stance for a configuration key (case-sensitive, as in QuickFIX/J).
pub fn key_info(name: &str) -> Option<&'static KeyInfo> {
    APPENDIX_A_KEYS.iter().find(|k| k.name == name)
}

# Dictionary source pipeline (stub — Stage S0)

This directory holds the **normalized TrueFix dictionary** inputs and the transform that produces them.
Per Constitution Principle III and spec FR-A2, dictionaries are **derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications (FIX Orchestra / Repository)** and normalized into
TrueFix's own format. QuickFIX/J `FIXxx.xml` files are **never** copied.

## Layout (to be populated in Stages S4/S7)

```
dict-src/
├── orchestra/      # vendored FIX Orchestra/Repository inputs (official, license-checked)
├── normalized/     # generated TrueFix normalized dictionaries (one per version)
└── generate.rs     # transform: orchestra -> normalized (invoked from build.rs)
```

## Dual-track usage (Principle IV)

The single `normalized/` output is consumed by:
- `build.rs` codegen → strongly-typed per-version messages (compile-time).
- the runtime `DataDictionary` validator (run-time).

A build assertion (T047) verifies both originate from the same normalized version/hash so they cannot
diverge.

## Status

S0 scaffold only. Population of `orchestra/` + the `generate` transform is tracked by tasks T048, T050,
T074, T075.

# TrueFix Codebase — Exhaustive Analysis (current state, no git history)

I read the actual source of **all 84 `.rs` files** under `crates/`, all 9 crate `Cargo.toml`s, the workspace `Cargo.toml`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `README.md`. Below is the complete current state.

## Workspace overview
- **9 crates**: `truefix-core`, `truefix-dict`, `truefix-session`, `truefix-store`, `truefix-log`, `truefix-transport`, `truefix-config`, `truefix-at`, `truefix` (facade). All `version = "0.0.0"`, `publish = false`, edition 2021, MSRV 1.96.
- Workspace lints: `unsafe_code = "forbid"`; each lib.rs adds `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds.
- Shared deps: tokio(full), tokio-rustls 0.26, rustls 0.23, thiserror 2, rust_decimal, time 0.3, sqlx 0.8 (sqlite, optional), async-trait, tracing.
- NOTE: README badge says "pre-implementation (design complete)" but the code is **substantially implemented** through roughly S0–S9.

## (a) truefix-core — message model + SOH codec
Files: `lib.rs, error.rs, field.rs, field_map.rs, group.rs, message.rs, codec/{mod,decode,encode}.rs, framing.rs, cracker.rs, factory.rs, tags.rs` + `benches/codec.rs`.
- **`Field`** (`field.rs`): tag + raw `Vec<u8>` value (verbatim for round-trip). Typed accessors `as_str/as_int/as_decimal/as_char/as_bool/as_utc_timestamp`, all returning `Result<_, FieldError>` (never panic). Timestamp parser accepts `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS[.fraction]`, picosecond input truncated to nanosecond.
- **`FieldError`**: NotUtf8/NotInt/NotDecimal/NotChar/NotBool/NotTimestamp. **`DecodeError`**: Empty, MissingBeginString, InvalidBodyLength, BodyLengthMismatch, MissingChecksum, ChecksumMismatch, GarbledField{offset,reason}, Truncated, InvalidTag.
- **`FieldMap`** (`field_map.rs`): ordered `Vec<Member>` where `Member = Field | Group{count_tag, entries: Vec<FieldMap>}`. Methods: `add_field`, `set` (replace/append), `get`, `contains`, `add_group`, `group`, `fields()`, `members()` (pub(crate)).
- **`Group`** (`group.rs`): count_tag + entries builder. NOTE: this lets you *build/encode* groups; **dictionary-driven *parsing* of groups from a flat wire message is NOT implemented** (core doc-comment defers it to truefix-dict Stage S4; dict's `validate.rs` likewise only walks flat `fields()`).
- **`Message`**: header/body/trailer `FieldMap`s. `encode()`/`decode()` delegate to codec. Canonical emit order `8,9,35,<header>,<body>,<trailer except 10>,10`.
- **codec/decode.rs**: tokenizes `tag=value<SOH>`, honors length-prefixed binary data fields (via `tags::data_field_for_length`), verifies BodyLength + CheckSum. Routes fields to header/body/trailer via `is_header`/`is_trailer`.
- **codec/encode.rs**: computes BodyLength + CheckSum, recurses into nested groups.
- **framing.rs** (`frame_length`): stream framing via BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer.
- **cracker.rs**: `MessageCracker` trait — **skeleton only, NOT typed dispatch**. Just `fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`. Doc says typed dispatch arrives with codegen Stage S4.
- **factory.rs**: `MessageFactory` trait — **skeleton only** (`fn create(&self, begin_string, msg_type) -> Message`).
- **tags.rs**: SOH=0x01, BEGIN_STRING=8, BODY_LENGTH=9, MSG_TYPE=35, CHECK_SUM=10, MSG_SEQ_NUM=34, SENDER_COMP_ID=49, TARGET_COMP_ID=56, SENDING_TIME=52. `is_header`/`is_trailer` classification + `data_field_for_length` map (SecureDataLen→SecureData, RawDataLength→RawData, XmlDataLen→XmlData, Encoded*Len→Encoded* pairs).

## (b) truefix-dict — dual-track data dictionary
Files: `lib.rs, build.rs, model.rs, validate.rs, parser.rs, fixt.rs, hash.rs` + `dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict` (9 dict files + README).
- **Versions supported**: FIX 4.0, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, **FIXT 1.1**, FIX 5.0, 5.0SP1, 5.0SP2. **NO "FIX Latest"** (no matches for fixlatest/FIX_Latest).
- **Build-time codegen** (`build.rs`): reads the 9 normalized `.fixdict` sources, emits per-version `{NAME}_DICT_HASH: u64` (FNV-1a, duplicated in `hash.rs`) + `pub mod {name}_msgs { pub const MSG_NAME: &str = ...; }` MsgType constants into `OUT_DIR/generated.rs`.
- **Runtime** (`parser.rs`): parses a custom normalized format — directives `version`, `field <tag> <name> <TYPE> [enumvals...]`, `header <tags>`, `trailer <tags>`, `message <msgtype> <name> [req:tag,tag] [opt:tag,tag]`, with `#` comments. `ParseError`: MissingVersion/Malformed{line}/UnknownType{line,token}.
- **`DataDictionary`** (`model.rs`): version, fields (BTreeMap<u32,FieldDef>), field_by_name, messages (BTreeMap<String,MessageDef>), header/trailer sets, hash. `FieldType` enum: Int/Length/SeqNum/NumInGroup/Float/Price/Qty/Amt/Percentage/Char/Boolean/String/Data/UtcTimestamp/UtcTimeOnly/UtcDateOnly/MonthYear. `FieldDef{tag,name,field_type,values}`. `MessageDef{msg_type,name,required,optional}`. **`RejectReason`** enum with codes: InvalidTagNumber(0), RequiredTagMissing(1), TagNotDefinedForMessageType(2), UndefinedTag(3), TagSpecifiedWithoutValue(4), ValueIsIncorrect(5), IncorrectDataFormat(6), InvalidMsgType(11). `ValidationError{reason,ref_tag,business,text}` (session vs business). `ValidationOptions`: validate_fields_have_values, validate_user_defined_fields, allow_unknown_msg_fields, check_required_fields, check_field_types.
- **validate.rs**: `DataDictionary::validate(&Message, &ValidationOptions)`. Walks flat header/body/trailer fields; UDFs (tag≥5000) skipped unless `validate_user_defined_fields`; unknown-msg-fields allowed if `allow_unknown_msg_fields`; checks value format, enum, field membership, required fields. Unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject; field failures → session reject. **Two rejection layers present.**
- **fixt.rs**: `FixtDictionaries` — transport dict + BTreeMap<appl_ver_id, DataDictionary> + default_appl_ver_id; `application_for()` resolves per-message.
- Tests: `versions.rs`, `parse.rs`, `fixt.rs`, `toggles.rs`, `rejection_layers.rs`, `dual_track.rs` (proves codegen hash == runtime hash).

## (c) truefix-session — session state machine
Files: `lib.rs, state.rs, application.rs, config.rs, session_id.rs, schedule.rs, admin.rs, tags.rs, time_util.rs`.
- **`Session`** (`state.rs`): **sans-IO** state machine. `Event` = Connected | Received(Message) | Tick | StartLogout. `Action` = Send(Message) | Disconnect. `SessionState` = Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout. `SessionStatus` snapshot (state, logged_on, next_sender_seq, next_target_seq).
- Implemented semantics: logon handshake (initiator sends Logon; acceptor adopts HeartBtInt, responds), heartbeat (sends HB when `ticks_since_send >= hb`, TestRequest when `ticks_since_recv > hb`, disconnect when `>= 2*hb+2`), TestRequest→Heartbeat echo, logout, **sequence-gap recovery**: in-order vs too-high (queue + ResendRequest) vs too-low (Logout+disconnect, or silent ignore if PossDup), **ResendRequest handling** (begin/end, begin=0 ignored, bounded end), **SequenceReset** (GapFill advances only if NewSeqNo≥expected; Reset always sets), out-of-order queue drain, chunked resend (`resend_request_chunk_size`), **NextExpectedMsgSeqNum(789)** (logon + immediate gap fill if behind), **LastMsgSeqNumProcessed(369)** stamping, **ResetSeqNumFlag(141)**, **CheckLatency/MaxLatency** (stale SendingTime → Logout+disconnect), logon/logout timeouts, reset_on_disconnect/logout, `set_dictionary()` for inbound app validation producing Reject/BusinessMessageReject. `seed_sequences()` for store continuity. `reset()` operational reset.
- **`Application`** trait (`application.rs`): async callbacks `on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app`. `from_admin` returning `Err` rejects the message (this is the **RejectLogon**-equivalent auth hook — no separate `ConnectionValidator`).
- **`SessionConfig`** (`config.rs`): begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval(30), role, reset_on_logon(true)/reset_on_logout(false)/reset_on_disconnect(false), refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size(0), enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed, check_latency(true), max_latency(120), logon_timeout(10), logout_timeout(10), reconnect_interval(5), schedule.
- **`SessionId`**: begin_string + sender/target + optional sub/location IDs + session_qualifier.
- **`Schedule`** (`schedule.rs`): non_stop / daily window (wraps past midnight) / weekday filter / whole-second UTC offset. `is_in_session(now_utc)`. Named TZ and StartDay/EndDay noted as future.
- **admin.rs**: builders for Logon/Logout/Heartbeat/TestRequest/ResendRequest/SequenceReset/Reject/`reject_with_reason`/`business_message_reject`/`prepare_resend` (stamps PossDupFlag + OrigSendingTime). **`time_util.rs`**: SendingTime always formatted at **millisecond** precision (`YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss`) — `TimeStampPrecision` key is Recognized but NOT honored.
- Tests: `state_machine.rs`, `recovery.rs`, `validation_hook.rs`, `timeouts.rs`, `schedule.rs`.

## (d) truefix-store — persistence
Files: `lib.rs, memory.rs, file.rs, noop.rs, sql.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default; sqlx sqlite).
- **`MessageStore`** async trait: `next_sender_seq`, `next_target_seq`, `set_next_sender_seq`, `set_next_target_seq`, `save(seq, &[u8])`, `get(begin, end) -> Vec<(u64, Vec<u8>)>`, `reset`. `StoreError` = Io | Backend.
- **`MemoryStore`**: `Mutex<State{sender,target,messages:BTreeMap}>`.
- **`FileStore`** (`file.rs`): `seqnums` file (sender/target on lines 1/2) + `body` append log (`seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`). On open replays body into memory; **tolerates/recoveres truncated/corrupt trailing record** and flags it via `was_corrupted()` (supports **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore** T060 — Recognized in keys, partial impl: corruption tolerated, good prefix recovered). **`CachedFileStore`** wraps FileStore (shared impl; "write-batching optimizations will live" here — not yet differentiated).
- **`NoopStore`**: always reports seq 1, discards saves.
- **`SqlStore`** (`sql.rs`, behind `sql` feature): SQLite via sqlx. Tables **hardcoded**: `seqnums` (id=0 singleton, sender/target), `messages` (seq PK, data BLOB). `StoreConfig::Sql{url}` + `build_store()`. **Table names NOT configurable** (Jdbc*TableName keys are Recognized but not honored); **no connection max-lifetime/idle/keepalive config** (JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime etc. Recognized but not honored — uses plain `SqlitePool::connect_with`).
- Tests: `restart_resend.rs`. **`FileStoreSync` key is Recognized but NOT honored** (FileStore uses synchronous `fs::write`, no fsync/sync toggle).

## (e) truefix-log — logging
Files: `lib.rs, screen.rs, file.rs, tracing_log.rs, composite.rs, sql.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default; sqlx sqlite + tokio).
- **`Log`** trait (sync, infallible): `on_incoming(&str)`, `on_outgoing(&str)`, `on_event(&str)` — message/event stream separation.
- Backends: **`ScreenLog`** (stdout msgs, stderr events), **`FileLog`** (`messages.log`+`event.log` in a dir), **`TracingLog`** (tracing facade, targets `truefix::message`/`truefix::event`), **`CompositeLog`** (fan-out), **`SqlLog`** (behind `sql` feature: writes to hardcoded `log_messages`/`log_events` tables via a background tokio task fed by an unbounded mpsc — bridges sync trait to async sqlx). `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`.
- Tests: `composite.rs`, `sql_log.rs`. **SLF4J keys are Unsupported** ("TrueFix logs via tracing facade; SLF4J category keys not applicable").

## (f) truefix-transport — TCP/TLS initiator+acceptor
Files: `lib.rs, framing.rs` (transport has its own `frame_length`, duplicate of core's).
- **Initiator**: `connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with` (services), `connect_initiator_tls` (rustls `ClientConfig` + SNI `ServerName`), **`connect_initiator_reconnecting`** (auto-reconnect after `reconnect_interval`), **`run_scheduled_initiator`** (connects only while `Schedule.is_in_session`; on entering window resets store, on leaving logs out).
- **Acceptor**: simple `Acceptor` (one static config) + **`AcceptorBuilder`** (multi-session): `with_session` (static by SessionID), `with_dynamic_template` (dynamic sessions), `allow_remotes` (IP allow-list), `with_tls` (rustls `ServerConfig`), `with_services`. Routing by SessionID: reads first complete message (Logon), reverses comp IDs to look up config, falls back to dynamic template.
- **`Services`**: optional `store` (Arc<dyn MessageStore>), `log`, `socket_options` (tcp_no_delay only), `monitor`, `validator` (DataDictionary + ValidationOptions). On connect: restores seq from store, registers with monitor. Persists seq numbers best-effort after each dispatch.
- **`Monitor`**: live status (`status`, `is_connected`, `sessions`), plus control actions `force_logout`, `reset`, `send_app` — described in code as **"the JMX-equivalent surface"** (no actual JMX/MBean/JmxExporter).
- `Application::from_admin` Err tears the session down (auth/reject-logon). Garbled frames silently dropped ("RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" — not yet). `SessionHandle` control: `logout`, `send`, `join`.
- Tests: `scheduled.rs`, `monitor.rs`, `auth.rs`, `tls.rs`, `multi_dynamic.rs`, `restart_continuity.rs`, `reconnect.rs`, `integration_logon.rs`.
- **TLS**: caller supplies pre-built `Arc<rustls::ClientConfig/ServerConfig>`; transport does NOT parse `EnabledProtocols`/cipher/keystore itself (those keys are marked Implemented in keys.rs but no parsing in transport — min-version/raw-bytes config not wired).

## (g) truefix-config — .cfg parsing + Appendix A registry
Files: `lib.rs, keys.rs`.
- **`SessionSettings::parse`**: QuickFIX-style `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` dialect, `#` comments, per-session override of defaults, **`${name}` interpolation** (errors on unresolved). Returns default section + resolved session sections. NOTE: **parses only — there is NO mapping from settings → `SessionConfig`** yet.
- **`keys.rs`**: `APPENDIX_A_KEYS` registry (~150 keys) with `Stance` = `Implemented`/`Recognized`/`Unsupported(&'static reason)`. Groups: identity, validation, session, scheduling, acceptor, initiator, socket, ssl, proxy, file-store, file-log, screen-log, facade-log, sql, sleepycat. `key_info(name)` lookup. Test `key_coverage.rs` asserts ≥140 keys, no dups, every Unsupported has a non-empty reason.
- **Proxy keys** (ProxyType/ProxyHost/…): all **Unsupported** ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy not yet implemented"). **Sleepycat keys**: all **Unsupported** ("deferred for v1"). **SLF4J keys**: Unsupported. **JNDI keys**: Unsupported ("not applicable in Rust").

## (h) truefix-at — acceptance test suite
Files: `lib.rs, runner.rs, scenarios.rs` + `tests/conformance.rs`.
- **`runner.rs`**: black-box driver. Starts a real `AcceptorBuilder` acceptor (dynamic template, optional validator/executor app) on `127.0.0.1:0`, scripts a TCP client. `Step` = Send(Message)/SendRaw(bytes)/Expect(ExpectMsg{msg_type, fields})/ExpectDisconnect. `Scenario{name, versions, steps, tweaks: SessionTweaks}`. `SessionTweaks`: enable_next_expected, enable_last_processed, check_latency, resend_chunk_size, executor_app (replies to NewOrderSingle with ExecutionReport). `run_report()` runs each scenario × each target version, fresh acceptor per (scenario,version). Validator only loaded for "FIX.4.2" and "FIX.4.4".
- **`SUITE_VERSIONS = ["FIX.4.2", "FIX.4.4"]`** — AT scenarios run ONLY against these two versions (not all 9).
- **`scenarios.rs` — exact scenario list (45 unique, 66 scenario×version instances)**:
  - **Shared, run vs BOTH FIX.4.2 & FIX.4.4 (21 × 2 = 42)**: `1a_ValidLogonWithCorrectMsgSeqNum`, `1a_ValidLogonMsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2b_MsgSeqNumTooHigh`, `2c_MsgSeqNumTooLow`, `4b_ReceivedTestRequest`, `13b_UnsolicitedLogoutMessage`, `1d_LogonResponseResetFlag`, `1c_LogonAdoptsHeartBtInt`, `2f_ResendRequestGapFill`, `2f3_ResendRequestBoundedEnd`, `2_ResendRequestNotDuplicated`, `2_ResendRequestBeginZeroIgnored`, `2x_OutOfOrderQueuedThenDrained`, `2g_SequenceResetReset`, `2d_SequenceResetGapFillAdvances`, `2e_SequenceResetGapFillBackwardIgnored`, `2h_ResendRequestNothingToResend`, `3a_ReceivedRejectConsumed`, `0a_ReceivedHeartbeatConsumed`, `2_MissingMsgSeqNum`, `2m_PossDupMsgSeqNumTooLow`.
  - **FIX.4.2 only (7)**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted_42`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing_42`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue_42`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber_42`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType_42`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue_42`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat_42`.
  - **FIX.4.4 only — field-validation + app (12)**: `14_valid_NewOrderSingleAccepted`, `app_NewOrderSingleExecuted`, `app_OrdersOutboundSequenced`, `app_MessageResentAsPossDup`, `app_MixedResendGapFillThenPossDup`, `14b_RequiredFieldMissing`, `14e_IncorrectEnumValue`, `14a_InvalidTagNumber`, `14c_TagNotDefinedForMsgType`, `14d_TagSpecifiedWithoutValue`, `14f_IncorrectDataFormat`, `2r_UnregisteredMsgType`.
  - **FIX.4.4 only — special suite (5)**: `special_NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, `special_LastMsgSeqNumProcessed`, `special_CheckLatencyStaleSendingTime`, `special_GarbledMessageDropped`, `special_ResendRequestChunkSize`.
- Categories: logon/sequencing, admin handling, resend/gap-fill, out-of-order queueing, field-validation reject family (14a–14f across 4.2 & 4.4), business reject (2r), app-execution/resend-as-PossDup, and special toggles (789/369, CheckLatency, garbled/checksum, chunk size).

## (i) truefix (facade)
`lib.rs` re-exports: core (`decode,encode,DecodeError,Field,FieldError,FieldMap,Group,Message,MessageCracker,MessageFactory`), session (`Action,Application,Event,Role,Session,SessionConfig,SessionId,SessionState`), transport (`connect_initiator,Acceptor,SessionHandle`), and `config` module. `#![deny(missing_docs)]`.
- **Examples (4)** in `crates/truefix/examples/`: `executor.rs` (acceptor that fills each NewOrderSingle with ExecutionReport, port 9881), `banzai.rs` (initiator client: logon→send NewOrderSingle→print ExecutionReport→logout), `multi_acceptor.rs` (two static sessions + dynamic template, port 9882), `ordermatch.rs` (simplified matching engine: per-symbol book, crossing orders filled on both sides, port 9883). Plus `tests/examples_smoke.rs`.

## Section 5 — specific feature checklist (verified by reading + grep)
- **failover/alternate/SocketConnectHost1/multi-endpoint/BackupHost**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches). Only single `SocketConnectHost`/`SocketConnectPort`.
- **proxy / PROXY protocol**: config keys present, **all Unsupported** ("HTTP/SOCKS proxy not yet implemented"). No PROXY-protocol support.
- **mongo/MongoDB**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches).
- **Sleepycat/berkeley**: keys present, **all Unsupported** ("deferred for v1; use file or SQL store").
- **fixlatest / FIX Latest**: **NOT PRESENT**. Versions are FIX 4.0–4.4, FIXT 1.1, FIX 5.0/SP1/SP2 only.
- **DoNotSend/RejectLogon/FieldNotFound (by name)**: not present as named concepts. RejectLogon behavior exists via `Application::from_admin` returning `Err` (tears session down). FieldNotFound maps to `RejectReason::InvalidTagNumber`/`RequiredTagMissing`.
- **UnsupportedMessageType/IncorrectTagValue**: map to `RejectReason::InvalidMsgType(11)` and `ValueIsIncorrect(5)`; `IncorrectDataFormat(6)` also exists.
- **MessageCracker**: **trait skeleton only, NOT typed dispatch** (cracker.rs doc says typed dispatch arrives with codegen Stage S4).
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter / ApplicationExtended**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **JMX/JmxExporter/MBean**: **NOT PRESENT** as actual JMX. `Monitor` is described in-code as "the JMX-equivalent surface" (status + force_logout/reset/send_app).
- **OSGi/bundle**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **ResetSeqTime**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **TimeStampPrecision**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT honored** — `time_util` always emits millisecond precision.
- **FileStoreSync**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT honored** — FileStore uses synchronous `fs::write`, no fsync/sync toggle.
- **HeartBtIntOverride**: **NOT PRESENT** (acceptor does adopt the peer's HeartBtInt from the Logon, but no override key).
- **ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore**: Recognized in keys.rs; **partial implementation** — FileStore detects corrupt trailing record, recovers the good prefix, exposes `was_corrupted()` (T060). No "force resend" behavior beyond recovery.
- **InChanCapacity**: **NOT PRESENT**.
- **ConnectionValidator**: **NOT PRESENT** (auth via `from_admin` Err instead).
- **watermark/backpressure**: **NOT PRESENT** (zero matches).
- **SLF4J**: keys present, **Unsupported** ("TrueFix logs via tracing facade; not applicable").
- **dictgenerator / codegen CLI**: **NO standalone CLI**. `build.rs` does build-time codegen (MsgType constants + FNV-1a hash) only; no `dictgenerator` binary.
- **custom SQL table names**: **NOT configurable** — SqlStore/SqlLog hardcode `seqnums`/`messages`/`log_messages`/`log_events`. Jdbc*TableName keys are Recognized but not honored.
- **SQL connection max lifetime**: JdbcMaxConnectionLifeTime Recognized, **NOT honored** (plain `SqlitePool::connect_with`).
- **TLS minimum version config / TLS raw bytes config**: `EnabledProtocols`/`CipherSuites`/`SocketKeyStore` etc. marked Implemented in keys.rs, but transport takes a **pre-built `Arc<rustls::ClientConfig/ServerConfig>`** from the caller — no protocol-version/cipher/keystore/PEM parsing in the transport crate.
- **virtual thread support**: N/A (Rust/tokio async; no such concept present).
- **ValidateUnorderedGroupFields**: Recognized in keys.rs, **NOT implemented** — group parsing from flat wire not yet done anywhere.
- **ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder**: Recognized, not implemented (decoder preserves wire order; no out-of-order rejection).

## Notable gaps / things not yet wired
1. **No settings→SessionConfig mapping** in truefix-config (parses `.cfg` only; engine uses programmatic `SessionConfig`).
2. **Group parsing from flat wire** deferred everywhere (core + dict validate walk flat fields only).
3. **RejectGarbledMessage** not implemented (garbled frames silently dropped in transport).
4. Many Appendix A keys are `Recognized` (parsed) but not behaviorally honored (TimeStampPrecision, FileStoreSync, FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs, RejectGarbledMessage, ResetOnError, PersistMessages, ClosedResendInterval, SendRedundantResendRequests, several socket/SSL options, all Jdbc tuning keys, screen-log toggles).
5. **No standalone dictgenerator CLI**, no JMX, no proxy, no Sleepycat, no MongoDB, no failover/multi-endpoint — all explicitly absent or Unsupported-with-reason.

## Evidence basis
All findings are from reading the current source: every `src/*.rs` in all 9 crates, all 9 `Cargo.toml`s, workspace `Cargo.toml`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`, `truefix-config/tests/key_coverage.rs`, plus grep verification across `crates/` for failover/mongo/fixlatest/JMX/OSGi/proxy/Sleepycat/Validate*/FileStoreSync/TimeStampPrecision/ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore. No git history was consulted.
args.next_steps: The codebase is fully mapped. If the caller wants to act on this: (1) the largest functional gaps are settings→SessionConfig wiring, flat-wire group parsing, and honoring the many Recognized-but-not-implemented config keys (TimeStampPrecision, FileStoreSync, RejectGarbledMessage, Jdbc tuning, etc.); (2) explicitly-absent QF/J features (failover/multi-endpoint, proxy/PROXY protocol, MongoDB, Sleepycat, JMX, OSGi, ApplicationExtended, dictgenerator CLI) are documented Unsupported or simply missing — these would be net-new work, not wiring. No further file reading is required to begin any of these.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.
